world.name: AI Sovereignty and Compute Access Fragmentation
world.domain: AI governance, semiconductor controls, cloud infrastructure, and cross-border compute access
world.boundedDescription: A bounded Narrative World covering AI model governance, semiconductor export controls, cloud access restrictions, sovereign compute policy, and cross-border compliance signals that shape whether compute remains a neutral market input or becomes a governed strategic resource.
world.summary: The world begins with a mostly commercial assumption around AI compute access. Over 18 months, repeated policy, legal, market, and infrastructure artifacts accumulate into a structurally governed compute environment, reclassifying AI capacity from commercial availability into a fragmented sovereignty regime.

events.label: T0
events.title: Baseline Commercial Compute Access Assumption
events.description: Baseline operating state in which AI compute is still treated primarily as a commercial cloud and semiconductor availability problem rather than a sovereignty or alignment control problem.

events.label: A1
events.title: Cloud Sovereignty Procurement Guidance
events.description: Public-sector procurement guidance introduces preference language for sovereign cloud and domestic control over model training environments.

events.label: A2
events.title: Expanded Chip Export Control Guidance
events.description: Export-control clarifications increase the probability that advanced compute access will be interpreted through strategic restriction rather than pure market allocation.

events.label: A3
events.title: Foundation Model Risk Registry Proposal
events.description: A proposal to register high-capability models adds early administrative structure around compute, model capability, and infrastructure governance.

events.label: A4
events.title: Cross-Border GPU Leasing Restriction Debate
events.description: Debate emerges around whether foreign leasing of advanced GPU capacity creates alignment and enforcement blind spots.

events.label: A5
events.title: Strategic Compute Capacity Disclosure Requirement
events.description: Operators of large training clusters are required to disclose material capacity and high-scale compute concentration above reporting thresholds.

events.label: A6
events.title: Sovereign AI Compute Reserve Announcement
events.description: A major jurisdiction announces a sovereign compute reserve, signaling that strategic model capacity is no longer treated as an interchangeable commercial input.

events.label: A7
events.title: Hyperscaler Region Locking Policy
events.description: A hyperscaler adds region-locked model training and checkpoint handling language for certain cross-border enterprise workloads.

events.label: A8
events.title: General-Purpose AI Obligations Enter Force
events.description: Staged governance obligations for general-purpose AI models begin to apply, tightening operational expectations around capability governance and supply chain accountability.

events.label: A9
events.title: Model Export Screening Mechanism Leak
events.description: A leaked screening mechanism suggests future review of high-capability model export and cross-border model serving arrangements.

events.label: A10
events.title: Compute Access Risk Added to Vendor Assessments
events.description: Enterprise and financial due-diligence frameworks begin to add compute access and jurisdictional dependency risk to supplier review models.

events.label: A11
events.title: Advanced Packaging Supply Shock
events.description: A packaging and interconnect supply shock reinforces the narrative that advanced AI capacity is vulnerable to concentrated geopolitical chokepoints.

events.label: A12
events.title: Retaliatory Cloud Localization Mandate
events.description: A retaliatory localization response forces certain model training and inference workloads into jurisdiction-specific infrastructure channels.

events.label: A13
events.title: AI Infrastructure Insurance Repricing
events.description: Insurance and contractual terms begin to reflect persistent jurisdictional fragmentation and elevated operational dependence on bounded compute pathways.

events.label: A14
events.title: Bloc-Aligned Compute Access Doctrine
events.description: A formal doctrine links AI compute access, sovereign alignment, and trusted infrastructure, locking in a fragmented cross-border operating environment.
